result:
Verification complete. Everything checks out:

**Script** — runs clean (exit 0, ~2s). Okabe-Ito palette + `theme_amce` declared at top, `set.seed(20260711)` before estimation, fully self-contained. Uses projoint's own estimator (`projoint(..., .estimand="amce")`) with the measurement-error-**corrected** AMCE, and `.auto_cluster` confirmed at runtime (`stopifnot(fit$cluster_by == "id")`) so SEs cluster at the respondent level.

**Figure** — `figures/amce-dotwhisker.png`, 2700×2700 (300 dpi). Meets every spec item: faceted/grouped by attribute, levels ordered within attribute, reference levels pinned at 0 and labeled "(ref)", 95% whiskers, no in-plot title.

**Report numbers cross-checked against fresh `estimates.csv`** — all exact:

| Claim | Report | Estimator |
|---|---|---|
| Violent crime +20% | −25.1 pp (−33.4, −16.8) | −0.251 (−0.334, −0.168) ✓ |
| Commute 75 min | −23.7 (−31.0, −16.5) | −0.237 (−0.310, −0.165) ✓ |
| Housing 40% | −19.8 (−27.4, −12.2) | −0.198 (−0.274, −0.122) ✓ |
| School 9/10 | +11.6 | +0.116 ✓ |
| Small town / mixed suburb / rural | +15.8 / +14.6 / +13.5 | +0.158 / +0.146 / +0.135 ✓ |

Partisanship and racial composition CIs all cross zero, matching the report's "indistinguishable from zero." The uncertainty sentence accurately describes respondent-clustered analytical SEs.

One honest footnote worth surfacing: projoint's CR2 cluster SEs are non-PSD on this data, so it falls back to `stata` (CR1) cluster-robust SEs — still respondent-clustered analytical SEs, consistent with the report's wording.

## Outcome

**Delegations used: 0 of 3.** Verification passed on the first run, so no fast-worker fix and no revision cycle were needed — spending a delegation on work that turned out correct would have been waste. All three deliverables (`script.R`, `figures/amce-dotwhisker.png`, `report.md`) are present, correct, and mutually consistent in the working directory. No parallel Opus∥Codex path was warranted: the task was low-blast-radius and fully verifiable by execution.
